testimonials.title: What Our Clients Say
testimonials.subtitle: Real stories from families and individuals we have helped

t1.name: Fatima A.
t1.text: Attorney Bachikh handled my green card case with incredible care and professionalism. He was always available to answer my questions and made a very stressful process feel manageable. I am now a permanent resident thanks to him!

t2.name: Carlos M.
t2.text: I was facing deportation and felt hopeless. Omar fought hard for my case and we won! He is not just a lawyer, he truly cares about his clients. I highly recommend him to anyone dealing with immigration issues.

t3.name: Aisha B.
t3.text: From the initial consultation to the approval notice, the process was smooth and transparent. Mr. Bachikh guided us through every step of my husband's naturalization. We are forever grateful!

t4.name: Jean-Pierre L.
t4.text: I needed help with my asylum case. Omar was knowledgeable, compassionate, and strategic. He prepared me thoroughly for my interview. I was granted asylum and finally feel safe. Thank you from the bottom of my heart.

t5.name: Yusuf H.
t5.text: Excellent service! The office communicated in Arabic which made everything so much easier for me. My case was handled professionally and I received my green card without any issues.

whyUs.title: Why Choose Our Firm
whyUs.subtitle: Experience, dedication, and results—these are the hallmarks of our representation
whyUs.item1Desc: With over a decade of focused immigration practice, we have the knowledge and strategy to handle even the most complex cases.
whyUs.item2Desc: Every client receives direct access to Attorney Bachikh, not a paralegal. Your case gets the individual attention it deserves.
whyUs.item3Desc: We serve clients in English, Arabic, and French—ensuring you can communicate fully about your case without barriers.
whyUs.item4Desc: We keep you informed at every step. No surprises, no hidden fees. Just honest, clear communication throughout your case.
whyUs.item5Desc: We approach every case with the rigor and preparation it deserves, building the strongest possible argument on your behalf.
whyUs.item6Desc: Deeply embedded in the Central Florida immigrant community, we understand the unique challenges our clients face.

about.pageSubtitle: Your trusted immigration advocate in Central Florida
about.heroText: Omar M. Bachikh is a dedicated immigration attorney with over 14 years of experience helping individuals and families navigate the complexities of U.S. immigration law. Based in Orlando, Florida, he has built a reputation for personalized service, strategic representation, and genuine care for every client.

bio.title: Attorney Omar M. Bachikh, Esq., LLM
bio.p1: Born with a deep understanding of the immigrant experience, Attorney Bachikh brings both legal expertise and personal empathy to every case. His multilingual capabilities—English, Arabic, and French—allow him to serve a diverse clientele spanning the Arab world, North Africa, francophone communities, and beyond.
bio.p2: After completing his LLM at the prestigious University of Paris VIII, Omar dedicated his career to immigration law, recognizing the profound impact legal representation has on families' lives. He founded the Law Office of Omar Bachikh, LLC to provide high-quality, accessible immigration services to the Central Florida community.
bio.p3: Attorney Bachikh is known for his thoroughness, his direct communication style, and his unwavering commitment to achieving the best possible outcome for each client. He handles every case personally, ensuring that you always have access to the attorney who knows your case best.
bio.philosophy: "Immigration law is not just about paperwork—it is about people's lives, their families, and their futures. Every client deserves an attorney who fights as hard for them as they would fight for a family member."

philosophy.title: Our Philosophy
philosophy.p1: At the Law Office of Omar Bachikh, LLC, we believe that every immigrant deserves compassionate, high-quality legal representation regardless of their background or the complexity of their case.
philosophy.p2: We take the time to understand each client's unique situation, fears, and goals. We explain every step of the process clearly and keep clients informed throughout. We fight with the same dedication for every client—whether they are seeking a family reunion visa or defending against deportation.
philosophy.p3: Our multilingual practice ensures that language barriers never stand between a client and the legal help they need. We are proud to serve the diverse immigrant communities of Central Florida and beyond.

quiz.title: Visa Eligibility Quiz
quiz.subtitle: Answer a few questions to discover which immigration options may be right for you.
quiz.resultSubtitle: Based on your answers, here is what we recommend:
quiz.urgentNotice: Important: Based on your situation, time may be critical. Please contact us immediately.
quiz.disclaimer: This quiz is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal advice. Please schedule a consultation with Attorney Bachikh for personalized guidance.

q1.question: What brings you here today?
q1.a: I want a green card (permanent residence)
q1.b: I want to become a U.S. citizen
q1.d: I am seeking asylum or protection
q1.e: I am facing deportation or removal

q2_greencard.question: Do you have a U.S. citizen or permanent resident family member?
q2_greencard.a: Yes — spouse who is a U.S. citizen
q2_greencard.b: Yes — parent, child, or sibling who is a U.S. citizen
q2_greencard.c: Yes — family member who is a permanent resident
q2_greencard.d: No — I have a job offer or employer sponsor

q2_citizenship.question: Are you currently a U.S. permanent resident (green card holder)?
q2_citizenship.b: Yes — for 3 years, married to a U.S. citizen
q2_citizenship.d: No — I am not yet a permanent resident

q2_work.question: What best describes your work situation?
q2_work.a: I have a U.S. employer who wants to sponsor me
q2_work.b: I have extraordinary ability in my field (arts, sports, science, business)
q2_work.c: I am being transferred by my multinational company
q2_work.d: I am a treaty investor or entrepreneur

q2_asylum.question: When did you last enter the United States?
q2_asylum.c: I have not entered the U.S. yet
q2_asylum.d: I am currently outside the U.S.

q2_deportation.question: What is your current situation?
q2_deportation.a: I have received a Notice to Appear (NTA) in immigration court
q2_deportation.b: I have a final order of removal
q2_deportation.d: I received a denial or Notice of Intent to Deny
q2_deportation.e: I want to appeal a court decision

q3.question: Are you currently inside the United States?
q3.a: Yes — I am in the U.S. legally
q3.b: Yes — but my status has expired or is irregular
q3.c: No — I am outside the U.S.
q3.d: No — but I am a U.S. national or national of a visa waiver country

q4.question: Have you ever had any of the following?

q5.question: What is your current immigration status in the U.S.?

familyGreenCard.title: Family-Based Green Card
familyGreenCard.desc: Based on your answers, you may be eligible for a family-based green card. The process typically involves an I-130 petition, followed by adjustment of status (if in the U.S.) or consular processing. Attorney Bachikh can guide you through every step.
familyGreenCard.action: Schedule a consultation to begin your petition →

marriageGreenCard.title: Spousal Green Card / Marriage Visa
marriageGreenCard.desc: As the spouse of a U.S. citizen, you may qualify for an immediate relative green card—one of the fastest paths to permanent residence. We will prepare and file your case with precision.
marriageGreenCard.action: Start your marriage green card process →

citizenship.title: Naturalization — U.S. Citizenship
citizenship.desc: You may be ready to apply for U.S. citizenship. The process includes Form N-400, a biometrics appointment, an English/civics test, and an oath ceremony. We will make sure your application is perfect.

workVisa.title: Employment-Based Visa
workVisa.desc: You may qualify for an H-1B, L-1, O-1, or other work visa. Each visa has specific requirements. Attorney Bachikh will assess your qualifications and recommend the strongest path forward.

asylum.title: Asylum Application
asylum.desc: If you are fleeing persecution, you may be eligible for asylum. Time limits apply—you generally must apply within 1 year of entering the U.S. Don't wait. Contact us today.
asylum.action: Get urgent help with your asylum case →

deportationDefense.title: Deportation Defense
deportationDefense.desc: Your situation requires immediate legal attention. Attorney Bachikh has extensive experience representing clients in removal proceedings. The sooner we act, the more options are available to you.

studentVisa.title: Student Visa (F-1 / J-1)
studentVisa.desc: International students pursuing academic programs in the U.S. need an F-1 visa. We can help you with the application process, maintaining status, OPT, and other student visa matters.
studentVisa.action: Get help with your student visa →

general.title: Let's Discuss Your Options
general.desc: Your situation may involve multiple pathways or require a detailed case analysis. Attorney Bachikh offers consultations to review your specific circumstances and identify the best strategy.

faq.pageSubtitle: Answers to the most common immigration questions
faq.q1: How long does the immigration process take?
faq.a1: Processing times vary greatly depending on the type of case, USCIS processing backlogs, and individual circumstances. Family-based cases for immediate relatives can take 1-2 years; other categories may take 3-10+ years. We will give you realistic timelines during your consultation.
faq.q2: How much does immigration legal help cost?
faq.a2: Our fees vary by case type and complexity. We offer transparent fee structures and payment plans. All fees are discussed upfront during your initial consultation. There are no hidden charges.
faq.q3: Can I apply for a green card if I entered the U.S. illegally?
faq.a3: It depends on several factors, including whether you have a qualifying family member, how long you've been in the U.S., and whether any bars to adjustment apply. Some people qualify for waivers. This is exactly the kind of situation we analyze in our paid consultations.
faq.q4: What is the difference between a green card and citizenship?
faq.a4: A green card grants you permanent residence in the U.S.—you can live and work here indefinitely but remain a citizen of your home country. Citizenship, achieved through naturalization, gives you all the rights of a U.S. citizen, including the right to vote and a U.S. passport.
faq.q5: Do I need a lawyer for my immigration case?
faq.a5: While immigration law does not technically require you to have an attorney, having professional legal representation dramatically improves your chances of success. Mistakes on immigration forms can result in delays, denials, or even bars to future applications. The stakes are too high to go it alone.
faq.q6: What happens if my visa petition is denied?
faq.a6: A denial is not the end. Depending on the type of denial, we can file a motion to reopen or reconsider, appeal to the Board of Immigration Appeals (BIA), or explore alternative pathways. We review every denial for appealable errors.
faq.q7: Can I work in the U.S. while my green card application is pending?
faq.a7: If you have a pending I-485 (Adjustment of Status), you can apply for an Employment Authorization Document (EAD), which allows you to work legally while your green card is being processed.
faq.q8: What is DACA and who qualifies?
faq.a8: DACA (Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals) is a program that protects certain undocumented immigrants who came to the U.S. as children from deportation and provides work authorization. Requirements include being brought to the U.S. before age 16, having continuous residence since June 15, 2007, and meeting education or military criteria.
faq.q9: How do I qualify for asylum?
faq.a9: To qualify for asylum, you must demonstrate that you have suffered persecution or have a well-founded fear of persecution in your home country based on race, religion, nationality, political opinion, or membership in a particular social group. You generally must apply within 1 year of arrival.
faq.q10: What languages does your office serve?
faq.a10: Our office serves clients in English, Arabic, and French. Attorney Bachikh is fluent in all three languages, ensuring you can fully communicate about your case without language barriers.

fees.pageSubtitle: Transparent pricing and a clear roadmap for your case
fees.transparencyText: We believe you deserve to know what your case will cost and what to expect. Our fee structures are clear, and we discuss all costs during your initial consultation.
fees.processSubtitle: A step-by-step overview of how we handle your case
fees.step1Desc: We review your situation, explain your options, and outline a strategy. Consultation fee applies.
fees.step2Desc: We review and sign the retainer agreement. All fees are disclosed upfront.
fees.step3Desc: We provide a tailored checklist of required documents. Our team assists you throughout.
fees.step4Desc: We prepare and file all required forms and supporting documentation with USCIS or the immigration court.
fees.step5Desc: We track your case, respond to any Requests for Evidence (RFEs), and keep you informed.
fees.step6Desc: For cases requiring an interview, we thoroughly prepare you for what to expect.
fees.step7Desc: We work diligently to achieve the best possible outcome and celebrate your success with you.
fees.feeNote: Actual fees are determined during your initial consultation based on your specific case. The following are general ranges for reference.
fees.feeDisclaimer: Government filing fees (USCIS fees) are separate from attorney fees and are set by USCIS. We will provide a complete cost breakdown before you commit.

results.pageSubtitle: Real outcomes for real people — a sample of the cases we have resolved
results.disclaimer: Note: Each case is unique. Prior results do not guarantee a similar outcome in your case.
results.r1Desc: Client from Morocco was reunited with her U.S. citizen husband after a 2-year separation. Complex consular processing case with prior visa denial successfully resolved.
results.r2Desc: Client facing deportation after an expired visa was able to adjust status through a family petition. Removal case terminated.
results.r3Desc: Client from Algeria who feared political persecution was granted asylum after a thorough presentation of country conditions evidence.
results.r4Desc: Client with a decades-old minor criminal record successfully naturalized after careful preparation and waiver application.
results.r5Desc: Software engineer's H-1B petition was approved on the first attempt despite the competitive lottery system.
results.r6Desc: DACA recipient's renewal and advance parole travel document approved, allowing them to visit family abroad for the first time in years.
